message:
fix(pt-redact): Atomic file permissions on key manager save

Fix a TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race condition when saving
the key manager file where the file could briefly exist with default
permissions before chmod was applied.

## Security Issue

The original code:
```rust
std::fs::write(&path, &content)?;  // Creates with default permissions
std::fs::set_permissions(&path, permissions)?;  // Then restricts
```

This creates a window where:
1. File is created with umask permissions (often 0644)
2. Key material is written to disk
3. Permissions are changed to 0600

During step 1-3, another process could read the key file.

## Fix

Use OpenOptions with mode() on Unix to create the file with restricted
permissions atomically:
```rust
std::fs::OpenOptions::new()
    .write(true)
    .create(true)
    .truncate(true)
    .mode(0o600)  // Permissions set at creation time
    .open(&path)?;
```

The kernel creates the file with 0600 permissions from the start,
eliminating the race window.

## Platform Handling

- Unix: Use OpenOptionsExt::mode() for atomic permission setting
- Non-Unix: Fall back to basic write (permissions model differs)

## Additional Safety

Added sync_all() after write to ensure content is flushed to disk
before the function returns, important for crash safety of key material.

## Impact

This fix affects KeyManager::save() which is called when:
- Rotating HMAC keys
- Initial key generation
- Key export operations

All paths that persist cryptographic key material now have this
protection against permission race conditions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
